message:
test: improve test infrastructure with local binary builds and temp dirs

This commit makes integration and E2E tests more reliable and hermetic by:
1. Building and using local ntm binary instead of relying on PATH
2. Using temp directories instead of user's home directory
3. Fixing flaky test patterns

BuildLocalNTM Enhancement (testutil/binary.go):
- Added findRepoRoot() to locate go.mod from any working directory
- Builds binary from repo root with proper error reporting
- Includes build output in error messages for debugging
- Handles Windows .exe suffix
- Changed from t.Skip to t.Fatal on build failure (tests should fail
  clearly, not silently skip)

RequireNTMBinary Enhancement (testutil/skip.go):
- Now calls BuildLocalNTM() to ensure local binary is available
- Prepends binary directory to PATH so exec.LookPath finds it
- Tests now run against workspace source, not globally installed binary
- Prevents stale binary issues and version mismatches

E2E Quick Tests Refactor (tests/e2e/quick_test.go):
- Added writeProjectsBaseConfig() helper to create temp config
- All tests now use t.TempDir() for projects_base via config
- No longer creates projects in ~/projects (was leaving test artifacts)
- Passes --config flag to ntm commands
- Cleanup is automatic via Go's temp directory management

Integration Config Tests Fixes (tests/integration/config_test.go):
- TestConfigCustomAgentCommands: Uses temp projects_base, unique session
  names, and AssertEventually for pane output verification
- TestConfigPaletteFromConfig: Sets XDG_CONFIG_HOME to temp dir to isolate
  from user's palette.md, fixed config format (key/label/prompt vs
  name/content)
- Properly finds cc pane by title pattern for output capture

These changes eliminate:
- Test pollution of user's ~/projects directory
- Dependency on globally installed ntm binary
- Race conditions in pane output capture
- Interference from user's existing configuration

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -11,31 +11,31 @@ import (
 	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/tests/testutil"
 )
 
+func writeProjectsBaseConfig(t *testing.T, projectsBase string) string {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	configPath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "config.toml")
+	content := fmt.Sprintf("projects_base = %q\n", projectsBase)
+	if err := os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to write config file: %v", err)
+	}
+	return configPath
+}
+
 // TestQuickSetupGo tests ntm quick with Go template creates correct scaffolding.
 func TestQuickSetupGo(t *testing.T) {
 	testutil.RequireNTMBinary(t)
 
 	logger := testutil.NewTestLoggerStdout(t)
 	projectName := fmt.Sprintf("ntm_e2e_test_go_%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
 
-	// Determine expected project path
-	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
-	if err != nil {
-		t.Fatalf("failed to get home dir: %v", err)
-	}
-	projectDir := filepath.Join(home, "projects", projectName)
-
-	// Clean up after test
-	t.Cleanup(func() {
-		logger.Log("CLEANUP: Removing %s", projectDir)
-		if err := os.RemoveAll(projectDir); err != nil {
-			t.Logf("cleanup warning: %v", err)
-		}
-	})
+	projectsBase := t.TempDir()
+	configPath := writeProjectsBaseConfig(t, projectsBase)
+	projectDir := filepath.Join(projectsBase, projectName)
 
 	// Run ntm quick with Go template
 	logger.LogSection("Creating Go project with ntm quick")
-	out := testutil.AssertCommandSuccess(t, logger, "ntm", "quick", projectName, "--template=go")
+	out := testutil.AssertCommandSuccess(t, logger, "ntm", "--config", configPath, "quick", projectName, "--template=go")
 	logger.Log("OUTPUT:\n%s", string(out))
 
 	// Verify project directory was created

 }

@@ -319,7 +279,7 @@ func TestQuickSetupInvalidName(t *testing.T) {

 		logger.LogSection(fmt.Sprintf("Testing invalid name: %s", name))
-		_ = testutil.AssertCommandFails(t, logger, "ntm", "quick", name)
